rxn_class_summary: Rhodium(III)-catalyzed electrooxidative C8–H alkenylation of 1-naphthols (and hydroxyquinolines) with styrenes under anodic conditions
electrochemical_role: Anode reoxidizes the reduced Rh(II) species (and Rh(III)→Rh(IV) en route to reductive elimination), generating H2 at the cathode; electricity acts beyond a simple sacrificial oxidant, with current-dependent rate indicating turnover-limiting electron transfer

key_intermediates.name: Cp*Rh(OPiv)2 / L-Rh(OPiv) active catalyst (species A precursor)
Five-membered peri-cyclorhodated naphtholate rhodacycle A (Cp*Rh(III) bound via O and C8)
Seven-membered alkyl–Rh(IV) rhodacycle C (after migratory insertion and anodic oxidation)
Cp*Rh(II) species D (post β-H elimination, prior to anodic reoxidation)

electron_flow: Rh(III) carboxylate performs concerted metalation–deprotonation at C8 of deprotonated 1-naphthol to form a rhodacycle; after styrene coordination and migratory insertion, anodic oxidation lifts Rh(III) to Rh(IV), enabling facile reductive elimination/β-H elimination to release the alkenylated product and Rh(II), which is reoxidized at the anode while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Turnover-limiting single-electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the Rh intermediate), supported by strong current dependence and negligible KIE

probe_reagents.name: D2O co-solvent (H/D exchange on 1-naphthol 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible C8 and C2 H/D scrambling with Rh catalyst (no alkene); only C2 scrambling without Rh; with alkene 2a, irreversible C–C bond formation and selective C8 functionalization

probe_reagents.name: Deuterated 1-naphthol (KIE study with 2e)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.1, indicating C–H metalation is fast and not rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: Variable current (CCE = 2, 4, 6 mA)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction rate scales strongly with current (ρ = 0.074, 0.102, 0.183), consistent with turnover-limiting ET

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Rh(III) (50 mol%) with 1a + 2l, no current
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3al obtained in only 40% yield, showing electricity has a role beyond sacrificial oxidation

notes: DFT (PW6B95-D4/def2-TZVP+SMD(i-PrOH)//PBE0-D3BJ/def2-SVP) compared Rh(III)/Rh(I) vs Rh(IV)/Rh(II) reductive elimination manifolds; the Rh(IV/II) pathway is preferred by 2.1 kcal/mol and gives a more stable Rh(II) intermediate (17.7 kcal/mol). The alternative Rh(III)/Rh(I) RE pathway is disfavored. Substrate 1d gave oxa-Michael and oxidative C–H/O–H cyclization products (pyranoquinolines 6) instead of alkenylation, indicating a competing cyclization pathway with hydroxyquinolines.
